title: prostanthera elisabethae
intro: Prostanthera elisabethae, also known as the Elizabethan mint bush, is a species of flowering plant in the mint family (Lamiaceae). It is native to Western Australia and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant has been traditionally used to treat a range of health issues, including colds, fever, and digestive problems, and its health benefits include relieving respiratory issues, reducing inflammation, and promoting wound healing. Prostanthera elisabethae can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or oil, and is also used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
